ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic croissant food product entry; no specific salted caramel croissant bites entry exists. Closest defensible match used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CJW covers croissant as a pastry category. No specific salted caramel variant code exists; closest defensible code used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for croissant bites or salted caramel pastry product. Left null to maintain ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of Salted Caramel Croissant Bites is approximately 60 g, equivalent to 5–6 individual bite-sized pieces, as commonly portioned in Singapore bakery and café settings.

nutrient_source.title: Salted Caramel Croissant Bites — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Salted Caramel Croissant Bites (cr_id: G00010285). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central croissant reference (USDA SR Legacy #18023) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item; estimated at approximately 9.5g/100g based on butter-laminated croissant dough composition.

gi_source.title: Croissant, plain — Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load values
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI value exists for Salted Caramel Croissant Bites in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 67 is taken from the closest analogous food: plain croissant (GI ~67, medium category) as published in the University of Sydney GI Database. The salted caramel coating adds refined sugar which may marginally elevate the effective GI; the value of 67 is therefore a conservative lower-bound estimate. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (67 × 45.8 × 0.60) / 100 = 18.4 g per 60 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 300 mg/100 g with low potassium offset (118 mg/100 g) and high estimated saturated fat (~9.5 g/100 g); salted caramel glaze likely adds further sodium beyond the reported figure.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should avoid or strictly limit Salted Caramel Croissant Bites, as the 300 mg/100 g sodium content — likely underestimated due to the salted caramel glaze — combined with high saturated fat and a poor potassium-to-sodium ratio contributes to elevated blood pressure and arterial stiffness.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High carbohydrate load (45.8 g/100 g) with medium GI (~67) yields a glycemic load of ~18.4 per 60 g serving; sugar at 11.26 g/100 g from caramel glaze further elevates postprandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid Salted Caramel Croissant Bites due to the high glycemic load (~18.4 per serving) driven by refined flour and caramel sugar, which causes rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; if consumed, portion must be strictly limited to no more than 30 g with a low-GI meal.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g/100 g from butter-laminated croissant dough, combined with cholesterol at 67 mg/100 g and sodium at 300 mg/100 g, presents a multi-pathway cardiovascular risk profile.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid Salted Caramel Croissant Bites, as the high saturated fat content (~9.5 g/100 g) raises LDL cholesterol, the 67 mg/100 g dietary cholesterol adds further atherogenic burden, and the elevated sodium contributes to hypertensive cardiovascular risk.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g/100 g from butter-laminated dough and refined caramel sugar promote pro-inflammatory cytokine activity (TNF-alpha, IL-6) relevant to RA disease activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid Salted Caramel Croissant Bites, as the high saturated fat from butter and refined sugar from caramel glaze are established dietary triggers for systemic inflammation that can exacerbate RA joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Salted Caramel Croissant Bites are bite-sized pieces of butter-laminated croissant pastry coated with a salted caramel glaze, sold as a snack or teatime treat in Singapore bakeries and cafés. The croissant base is made from a laminated dough of flour, butter, eggs, and yeast, producing a crispy, flaky exterior with a soft, layered interior. The salted caramel coating adds a sweet-salty flavour contrast and a slightly sticky, glossy finish. Per 100 g, the product provides 406 kcal, 8.2 g protein, 21.0 g fat (estimated saturated fat ~9.5 g from butter), 45.8 g carbohydrates, 11.26 g sugar, 2.6 g dietary fibre, and 300 mg sodium. The glycemic index is estimated at approximately 67 (medium) based on plain croissant analogy, yielding a glycemic load of ~18.4 per 60 g serving. Clinically, this product is high concern for Type 2 Diabetes due to its high glycemic load, high concern for hypertension due to elevated sodium and poor potassium offset, and high concern for cardiovascular disease due to saturated fat and cholesterol content. It is classified as a processed Western-style pastry dessert, suitable for occasional consumption only, and is not appropriate for individuals managing blood glucose, blood pressure, or cardiovascular risk without strict portion control.
common_user_queries: What are salted caramel croissant bites?
How many calories in salted caramel croissant bites?
Are croissant bites suitable for diabetics?
Sodium content of salted caramel croissant bites

Can I eat croissant bites if I have hypertension?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry (cr_id: G00010285) with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy croissant reference as HPB does not separately report this field. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database plain croissant analogy (GI ~67). All sources are approved under Project Veritas v2 source policy.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (9.5 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #18023 (croissant, butter) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this item. This is a close analogy given the butter-laminated dough composition. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific salted caramel croissant bites product are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 3. GI of 67 is an analogous estimate based on plain croissant from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement exists for salted caramel croissant bites. The caramel coating may marginally elevate the effective GI above this estimate. 4. Halal status is not confirmed; the product contains butter and eggs from unspecified sources. 5. Actual sodium content may be higher than 300 mg/100 g due to the salted caramel glaze component, which may not be fully captured in the base HPB record.
